1→package agent
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"log/slog"
     8→	"time"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    11→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    12→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// AgentWorker is a River worker that processes AgentJobArgs by running the
    16→// LLM agent orchestrator. When a job is dequeued, it loads the corresponding
    17→// agent run from the store and delegates to Orchestrator.RunAgent.
    18→type AgentWorker struct {
    19→	river.WorkerDefaults[queue.AgentJobArgs]
    20→	orchestrator *Orchestrator
    21→	store        OrchestratorStore
    22→}
    23→
    24→// NewAgentWorker creates a new AgentWorker backed by the given orchestrator
    25→// and store.
    26→func NewAgentWorker(orchestrator *Orchestrator, store OrchestratorStore) *AgentWorker {
    27→	return &AgentWorker{
    28→		orchestrator: orchestrator,
    29→		store:        store,
    30→	}
    31→}
    32→
    33→// Timeout overrides River's default 60-second job timeout. Agent runs involve
    34→// multiple LLM round-trips (root agent + sub-agents), each of which can take
    35→// 10-30 seconds, so we allow 5 minutes total.
    36→func (w *AgentWorker) Timeout(_ *river.Job[queue.AgentJobArgs]) time.Duration {
    37→	return 5 * time.Minute
    38→}
    39→
    40→// Work processes a single AgentJobArgs job. It loads the agent run from the
    41→// database and runs the LLM agent through the orchestrator. If the project
    42→// has WaitForAllSources enabled and not all sources have the target version,
    43→// the job is snoozed for 5 minutes via river.JobSnooze.
    44→func (w *AgentWorker) Work(ctx context.Context, job *river.Job[queue.AgentJobArgs]) error {
    45→	slog.Info("agent worker picked up job",
    46→		"job_id", job.ID,
    47→		"agent_run_id", job.Args.AgentRunID,
    48→		"attempt", job.Attempt,
    49→	)
    50→
    51→	run, err := w.store.GetAgentRun(ctx, job.Args.AgentRunID)
    52→	if err != nil {
    53→		slog.Error("agent worker failed to load agent run",
    54→			"job_id", job.ID,
    55→			"agent_run_id", job.Args.AgentRunID,
    56→			"err", err,
    57→		)
    58→		return fmt.Errorf("get agent run: %w", err)
    59→	}
    60→
    61→	// Check multi-source waiting: if the run has a version and the project
    62→	// has WaitForAllSources enabled, verify all sources have reported a
    63→	// release for the target version before running the agent.
    64→	if run.Version != "" {
    65→		project, err := w.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
    66→		if err != nil {
    67→			slog.Error("agent worker failed to load project",
    68→				"job_id", job.ID,
    69→				"project_id", run.ProjectID,
    70→				"err", err,
    71→			)
    72→			return fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
    73→		}
    74→
    75→		var rules models.AgentRules
    76→		if len(project.AgentRules) > 0 {
    77→			if err := json.Unmarshal(project.AgentRules, &rules); err != nil {
    78→				slog.Warn("agent worker: failed to unmarshal agent rules, proceeding without wait",
    79→					"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
    80→			}
    81→		}
    82→
    83→		if rules.WaitForAllSources {
    84→			ready, err := w.orchestrator.checkAllSourcesReady(ctx, run.ProjectID, run.Version)
    85→			if err != nil {
    86→				slog.Error("agent worker: error checking source readiness",
    87→					"job_id", job.ID,
    88→					"project_id", run.ProjectID,
    89→					"version", run.Version,
    90→					"err", err,
    91→				)
    92→				return fmt.Errorf("check all sources ready: %w", err)
    93→			}
    94→			if !ready {
    95→				// After maxWaitAttempts (12 × 5min = ~1 hour), proceed
    96→				// with partial data rather than waiting forever.
    97→				const maxWaitAttempts = 12
    98→				if job.Attempt >= maxWaitAttempts {
    99→					slog.Warn("agent worker: max wait attempts reached, proceeding with partial data",
   100→						"job_id", job.ID,
   101→						"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   102→						"version", run.Version,
   103→						"attempts", job.Attempt,
   104→					)
   105→				} else {
   106→					slog.Info("agent worker: not all sources ready, snoozing",
   107→						"job_id", job.ID,
   108→						"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   109→						"version", run.Version,
   110→						"attempt", job.Attempt,
   111→					)
   112→					if err := w.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "waiting"); err != nil {
   113→						slog.Error("failed to set waiting status", "err", err)
   114→					}
   115→					return river.JobSnooze(5 * time.Minute)
   116→				}
   117→			}
   118→		}
   119→	}
   120→
   121→	slog.Info("agent worker starting run",
   122→		"job_id", job.ID,
   123→		"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   124→		"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   125→		"trigger", run.Trigger,
   126→	)
   127→
   128→	if err := w.orchestrator.RunAgent(ctx, run); err != nil {
   129→		slog.Error("agent worker run failed",
   130→			"job_id", job.ID,
   131→			"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   132→			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   133→			"err", err,
   134→		)
   135→		return err
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	slog.Info("agent worker run completed",
   139→		"job_id", job.ID,
   140→		"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   141→		"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   142→	)
   143→	return nil
   144→}
   145→

1→package openai
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→
     6→	"google.golang.org/genai"
     7→
     8→	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
     9→	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// WebSearch is a tool that enables OpenAI's built-in web search via
    13→// web_search_options. It mirrors geminitool.GoogleSearch by adding a
    14→// GoogleSearch marker to the request config, which the OpenAI wrapper
    15→// translates into the web_search_options HTTP body field.
    16→type WebSearch struct{}
    17→
    18→// Name implements tool.Tool.
    19→func (WebSearch) Name() string { return "web_search" }
    20→
    21→// Description implements tool.Tool.
    22→func (WebSearch) Description() string {
    23→	return "Search the web for additional context about a release."
    24→}
    25→
    26→// IsLongRunning implements tool.Tool.
    27→func (WebSearch) IsLongRunning() bool { return false }
    28→
    29→// ProcessRequest implements toolinternal.RequestProcessor by adding a
    30→// GoogleSearch marker to the LLM request. The OpenAI model wrapper
    31→// detects this marker and emits web_search_options in the HTTP body.
    32→func (WebSearch) ProcessRequest(_ tool.Context, req *model.LLMRequest) error {
    33→	if req == nil {
    34→		return fmt.Errorf("llm request is nil")
    35→	}
    36→	if req.Config == nil {
    37→		req.Config = &genai.GenerateContentConfig{}
    38→	}
    39→	req.Config.Tools = append(req.Config.Tools, &genai.Tool{
    40→		GoogleSearch: &genai.GoogleSearch{},
    41→	})
    42→	return nil
    43→}

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"flag"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"log"
     8→	"log/slog"
     9→	"os"
    10→
    11→	"google.golang.org/adk/agent"
    12→	"google.golang.org/adk/cmd/launcher"
    13→	"google.golang.org/adk/cmd/launcher/full"
    14→
    15→	agentpkg "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent"
    16→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/api"
    17→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/db"
    18→)
    19→
    20→func main() {
    21→	projectID := flag.String("project-id", "", "Project ID to scope the agent to (required)")
    22→	flag.Parse()
    23→
    24→	// Configure log level from LOG_LEVEL env (debug, info, warn, error).
    25→	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info
    26→	switch os.Getenv("LOG_LEVEL") {
    27→	case "debug":
    28→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelDebug)
    29→	case "warn":
    30→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelWarn)
    31→	case "error":
    32→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelError)
    33→	}
    34→	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: logLevel})))
    35→
    36→	if *projectID == "" {
    37→		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "error: --project-id is required")
    38→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "usage: go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=<uuid> [web api webui]\n")
    39→		os.Exit(1)
    40→	}
    41→
    42→	ctx := context.Background()
    43→
    44→	// Database
    45→	dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
    46→	pool, err := db.NewPool(ctx, dbURL)
    47→	if err != nil {
    48→		log.Fatalf("database connection failed: %v", err)
    49→	}
    50→	defer pool.Close()
    51→
    52→	store := api.NewPgStore(pool, nil)
    53→
    54→	// Validate project exists
    55→	project, err := store.GetProject(ctx, *projectID)
    56→	if err != nil {
    57→		log.Fatalf("project %q not found: %v", *projectID, err)
    58→	}
    59→	slog.Info("loaded project", "id", project.ID, "name", project.Name)
    60→
    61→	// LLM config (same env vars as server)
    62→	llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
    63→	llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
    64→	if llmProvider == "openai" {
    65→		llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
    66→	}
    67→	llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    68→		Provider:          llmProvider,
    69→		Model:             envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    70→		GoogleAPIKey:      os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    71→		OpenAIAPIKey:      os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    72→		OpenAIBaseURL:     os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    73→		OpenAISearchModel: envOr("OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL", "gpt-5-search-api"),
    74→	}
    75→
    76→	agentInstance, err := agentpkg.BuildAgent(ctx, store, project, llmConfig, "")
    77→	if err != nil {
    78→		log.Fatalf("build agent: %v", err)
    79→	}
    80→
    81→	// Launch with ADK-Web UI
    82→	config := &launcher.Config{
    83→		AgentLoader: agent.NewSingleLoader(agentInstance),
    84→	}

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"net/http"
     7→	"os"
     8→	"os/signal"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    12→	agentpkg "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent"
    13→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/api"
    14→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/db"
    15→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
    16→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    17→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    18→)
    19→
    20→func main() {
    21→	// Configure log level from LOG_LEVEL env (debug, info, warn, error).
    22→	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info
    23→	switch os.Getenv("LOG_LEVEL") {
    24→	case "debug":
    25→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelDebug)
    26→	case "warn":
    27→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelWarn)
    28→	case "error":
    29→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelError)
    30→	}
    31→	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: logLevel})))
    32→
    33→	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt)
    34→	defer stop()
    35→
    36→	dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
    37→	addr := envOr("LISTEN_ADDR", ":8080")
    38→	noAuth := os.Getenv("NO_AUTH") == "true"
    39→
    40→	// Database
    41→	pool, err := db.NewPool(ctx, dbURL)
    42→	if err != nil {
    43→		slog.Error("database connection failed", "err", err)
    44→		os.Exit(1)
    45→	}
    46→	defer pool.Close()
    47→
    48→	if err := db.RunMigrations(ctx, pool); err != nil {
    49→		slog.Error("migrations failed", "err", err)
    50→		os.Exit(1)
    51→	}
    52→
    53→	// API & routing store (created before River so we can register workers)
    54→	// The river client is set to nil initially; TriggerAgentRun (which needs it)
    55→	// won't be called until the server is up and the client is set below.
    56→	pgStore := api.NewPgStore(pool, nil)
    57→
    58→	// Register River workers
    59→	workers := river.NewWorkers()
    60→	notifyWorker := routing.NewNotifyWorker(pgStore, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    61→	river.AddWorker(workers, notifyWorker)
    62→
    63→	// Agent worker: requires an LLM API key. If unavailable, agent jobs
    64→	// will remain in the queue until the key is configured and the server
    65→	// is restarted.
    66→	llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
    67→	llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
    68→	if llmProvider == "openai" {
    69→		llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
    70→	}
    71→	llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    72→		Provider:          llmProvider,
    73→		Model:             envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    74→		GoogleAPIKey:      os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    75→		OpenAIAPIKey:      os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    76→		OpenAIBaseURL:     os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    77→		OpenAISearchModel: envOr("OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL", "gpt-5-search-api"),
    78→	}
    79→	agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore, llmConfig, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    80→	if err != nil {
    81→		slog.Warn("agent orchestrator not available — agent jobs will not be processed", "err", err)
    82→	} else {
    83→		agentWorker := agentpkg.NewAgentWorker(agentOrchestrator, pgStore)
    84→		river.AddWorker(workers, agentWorker)
    85→		slog.Info("agent worker registered")
    86→	}
    87→
    88→	riverClient, err := queue.NewRiverClient(pool, workers)
    89→	if err != nil {
    90→		slog.Error("river client failed", "err", err)
    91→		os.Exit(1)
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	// Now that the river client exists, set it on the existing pgStore so that
    95→	// all holders (including NotifyWorker) can enqueue agent jobs.
    96→	pgStore.SetRiverClient(riverClient)
    97→
    98→	if err := riverClient.Start(ctx); err != nil {
    99→		slog.Error("river start failed", "err", err)
   100→		os.Exit(1)
   101→	}
   102→
   103→	// Ingestion layer
   104→	ingestionStore := ingestion.NewPgStore(pool, riverClient)
   105→	svc := ingestion.NewService(ingestionStore)
   106→
   107→	loader := ingestion.NewSourceLoader(pool, http.DefaultClient)
   108→	orch := ingestion.NewOrchestrator(svc, loader, pool, 5*time.Minute)
   109→
   110→	broadcaster := api.NewBroadcaster()
   111→
   112→	mux := http.NewServeMux()
   113→
   114→	// Serve frontend static export if available
   115→	if info, err := os.Stat("web/out"); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   116→		api.RegisterFrontend(mux, "web/out")
   117→		slog.Info("serving frontend from web/out")
   118→	}
   119→
   120→	// Register all API v1 routes
   121→	api.RegisterRoutes(mux, api.Dependencies{
   122→		DB:                    pool,
   123→		ProjectsStore:         pgStore,
   124→		ReleasesStore:         pgStore,
   125→		SubscriptionsStore:    pgStore,
   126→		SourcesStore:          pgStore,
   127→		ChannelsStore:         pgStore,
   128→		ContextSourcesStore:   pgStore,
   129→		SemanticReleasesStore: pgStore,
   130→		AgentStore:            pgStore,
   131→		KeyStore:              pgStore,
   132→		HealthChecker:         pgStore,
   133→		Broadcaster:           broadcaster,
   134→		NoAuth:                noAuth,
   135→		IngestionService:      svc,
   136→		HTTPClient:            http.DefaultClient,
   137→	})
   138→
   139→	srv := &http.Server{Addr: addr, Handler: api.CORS(mux)}
   140→
   141→	// Start polling in background
   142→	go orch.Run(ctx)
   143→
   144→	// Start PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY → SSE bridge
   145→	go api.ListenForNotifications(ctx, pool, broadcaster)
   146→
   147→	// Start HTTP server
   148→	go func() {
   149→		slog.Info("server starting", "addr", srv.Addr)
   150→		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   151→			slog.Error("server error", "err", err)
   152→		}
   153→	}()
   154→
   155→	<-ctx.Done()
   156→	slog.Info("shutting down")
   157→
   158→	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   159→	defer cancel()
   160→	riverClient.Stop(shutdownCtx)
   161→	srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
   162→}
   163→
   164→func envOr(key, fallback string) string {
   165→	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
   166→		return v
   167→	}
   168→	return fallback
   169→}

func (w *AgentWorker) Work(ctx context.Context, job *river.Job[queue.AgentJobArgs]) error {

### 14. **Graceful Degradation**

The system gracefully degrades when LLM API keys are unavailable:

1. **Server startup** (main.go, line 79-86):
   - `NewOrchestrator()` returns error if API key missing
   - Agent worker is NOT registered
   - Rest of system (ingestion, notifications) continues

2. **At runtime**:
   - Agent jobs remain in River queue unprocessed
   - Restart server with API key configured to resume

3. **Agent run failure**:
   - Marked as "failed" with error captured
   - JSON parse failure: falls back to storing raw text as summary

   203→		}
   204→	default: // gemini

   207→		}

   216→// RunAgent executes the full agent lifecycle for a given agent run:
   217→//  1. Marks the run as "running".
   218→//  2. Loads the project and builds the agent prompt.
   219→//  3. Creates an ADK-Go LLM agent with project-scoped tools.
   220→//  4. Runs the agent and captures its final text response.
   221→//  5. Parses the response into a SemanticReport.
   222→//  6. Creates a SemanticRelease and links it to the agent run.
   223→//  7. Marks the run as "completed" (or "failed" on error).
   224→func (o *Orchestrator) RunAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) error {
   225→	// Mark as running.
   226→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "running"); err != nil {
   227→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to running: %w", err)
   228→	}
   229→
   230→	// Execute the agent; capture any error to mark the run as failed.
   231→	result, err := o.executeAgent(ctx, run)
   232→	if err != nil {
   233→		slog.Error("agent run failed", "run_id", run.ID, "err", err)
   234→		// Best-effort: mark the run as failed.
   235→		if statusErr := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "failed"); statusErr != nil {
   236→			slog.Error("agent: failed to mark run as failed",
   237→				"run_id", run.ID, "status_err", statusErr)
   238→		}
   239→		return err
   240→	}
   241→
   242→	// Link the semantic release to the agent run and mark completed.
   243→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, run.ID, result.semanticReleaseID); err != nil {
   244→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run result: %w", err)
   245→	}
   246→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "completed"); err != nil {
   247→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to completed: %w", err)
   248→	}
   249→
   250→	// Send project-level notifications (best-effort; errors are logged, not returned).
   251→	o.sendProjectNotifications(ctx, run, result)
   252→
   253→	return nil
   254→}

   265→// executeAgent performs the actual LLM agent interaction and returns the
   266→// agentResult containing the semantic release ID and metadata for notifications.
   267→func (o *Orchestrator) executeAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) (*agentResult, error) {
   268→	// Load project.
   269→	slog.Info("agent: loading project", "run_id", run.ID, "project_id", run.ProjectID)
   270→	project, err := o.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   271→	if err != nil {
   272→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
   273→	}
   274→	slog.Info("agent: project loaded", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name)
   275→
   276→	// Extract target version from the agent run.
   277→	version := run.Version
   278→	if version == "" {
   279→		// Fallback: parse from trigger "auto:version:v1.10.15"
   280→		if strings.HasPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:") {
   281→			version = strings.TrimPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:")
   282→		}
   283→	}
   284→
   285→	// Build the agent using the shared constructor.
   286→	slog.Info("agent: building agent", "run_id", run.ID,
   287→		"provider", o.llmConfig.Provider, "model", o.llmConfig.Model,
   288→		"version", version)
   289→	agentInstance, err := BuildAgent(ctx, o.store, project, o.llmConfig, version)
   290→	if err != nil {
   291→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("build agent: %w", err)
   292→	}
   293→
   294→	// Create in-memory session service and a new session.
   295→	sessionService := session.InMemoryService()
   296→	createResp, err := sessionService.Create(ctx, &session.CreateRequest{
   297→		AppName: "changelogue",
   298→		UserID:  "system",
   299→	})
   300→	if err != nil {
   301→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create session: %w", err)
   302→	}
   303→	sess := createResp.Session
   304→
   305→	// Create the runner with logging plugin for agent trace visibility.
   306→	r, err := runner.New(runner.Config{
   307→		AppName:        "changelogue",
   308→		Agent:          agentInstance,
   309→		SessionService: sessionService,
   310→		PluginConfig: runner.PluginConfig{
   311→			Plugins:      []*plugin.Plugin{loggingplugin.MustNew("agent_trace")},
   312→			CloseTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
   313→		},
   314→	})
   315→	if err != nil {
   316→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create runner: %w", err)
   317→	}
   318→
   319→	// Run the agent with a prompt requesting analysis.
   320→	slog.Info("agent: starting LLM run", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name, "version", version)
   321→	var userPrompt string
   322→	if version != "" {
   323→		userPrompt = fmt.Sprintf("Analyze version %s for this project. Cross-check all sources and produce a semantic release report.", version)
   324→	} else {
   325→		userPrompt = "Analyze the recent releases for this project and produce a semantic release report."
   326→	}
   327→	userMsg := genai.NewContentFromText(userPrompt, "user")
   328→
   329→	var finalText string
   330→	eventCount := 0
   331→	for event, err := range r.Run(ctx, "system", sess.ID(), userMsg, agent.RunConfig{}) {
   332→		if err != nil {
   333→			slog.Error("agent: run event error",
   334→				"run_id", run.ID,
   335→				"event_count", eventCount,
   336→				"err", err,
   337→			)
   338→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent run event error: %w", err)
   339→		}
   340→		eventCount++
   341→		if event != nil && event.IsFinalResponse() && event.Content != nil {
   342→			for _, part := range event.Content.Parts {
   343→				if part.Text != "" {
   344→					finalText += part.Text
   345→				}
   346→			}
   347→		}
   348→	}
   349→	slog.Info("agent: LLM run finished",
   350→		"run_id", run.ID,
   351→		"event_count", eventCount,
   352→		"output_length", len(finalText),
   353→	)
   354→
   355→	if finalText == "" {
   356→		slog.Error("agent: produced no output",

   503→// It handles cases where the agent wraps the JSON in markdown code blocks.
   504→func parseReport(text string) (*models.SemanticReport, error) {
   505→	// Strip markdown code fences if present.
   506→	cleaned := strings.TrimSpace(text)
   507→	if strings.HasPrefix(cleaned, "```") {
   508→		// Remove opening fence (with optional language tag).
   509→		if idx := strings.Index(cleaned, "\n"); idx != -1 {
   510→			cleaned = cleaned[idx+1:]
   511→		}
   512→		// Remove closing fence.
   513→		if idx := strings.LastIndex(cleaned, "```"); idx != -1 {
   514→			cleaned = cleaned[:idx]
   515→		}
   516→		cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
   517→	}
   518→

   520→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(cleaned), &report); err != nil {
   521→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse report JSON: %w", err)
   522→	}

   524→	if report.Subject == "" && report.Summary == "" {
   525→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("report is missing required 'subject' or 'summary' field")
   526→	}
   527→
   528→	return &report, nil

Good. So the goal is to migrate the OpenAI adapter from the **Chat Completions API** to the **Responses API**, gaining access to the proper `web_search` tool with parameters like domain filtering, search context size, and user location.

Here are the approaches I see:

## Approach 1: Full Responses API migration (Recommended)

Replace the entire `internal/agent/openai/openai.go` adapter to call `/v1/responses` instead of `/v1/chat/completions`. This means:

- New request/response types matching the Responses API format (`input`, `tools: [{type: "web_search", ...}]`, output items)
- The `WebSearch` tool can now carry config (allowed_domains, search_context_size)
- No more need for a separate `OpenAISearchModel` — the same model handles search natively
- **Trade-off**: Larger change, touches the core adapter. The Responses API uses a different message format (`input` items vs `messages` array). But it's a clean break.

## Approach 2: Hybrid — add Responses API alongside Chat Completions

Keep the existing Chat Completions adapter for the main model, but add a second adapter that uses the Responses API specifically for web search calls. The search sub-agent would use the Responses API adapter.

- **Trade-off**: Two code paths to maintain, but lower risk since the main agent path is unchanged.

## Approach 3: Chat Completions with enhanced web_search_options

Stay on Chat Completions but enhance `web_search_options` with the parameters it does support (it supports `search_context_size` and `user_location` even on Chat Completions).

- **Trade-off**: Minimal change, but Chat Completions web search only works with special `-search-` models and has fewer features than the Responses API. Doesn't give you domain filtering.

**My recommendation is Approach 1** — a full migration to the Responses API. The Responses API is OpenAI's primary path forward, supports all models (no special search model needed), and gives full control over web search parameters. The Chat Completions search models are legacy. This also simplifies `LLMConfig` by removing `OpenAISearchModel`.

content.text:
## Design: Migrate OpenAI Adapter to Responses API

### Architecture

The core change is replacing `POST /v1/chat/completions` with `POST /v1/responses`. This changes the wire format but the ADK interface (`model.LLM`) stays the same.

**Request format changes:**
- `messages` array → `input` (string or array of input items)
- `tools: [{type: "function", function: {...}}]` → `tools: [{type: "function", ...}, {type: "web_search", ...}]`
- `web_search_options: {}` (separate field) → `tools: [{type: "web_search", search_context_size: "medium", user_location: {...}}]` (native tool)

**Response format changes:**
- `choices[0].message.content` → `output` array with `message` items
- `choices[0].message.tool_calls` → `output` items with `type: "function_call"`
- Annotations (citations) come embedded in `output_text.annotations`

### Key Components

1. **`openai.go`** — Rewrite `doRequest()` and all type definitions to use `/v1/responses` format
2. **`websearch.go`** — `WebSearch` tool now carries optional config (allowed_domains, search_context_size) that gets encoded directly into the `tools` array
3. **`model.go`** — Remove `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig` since any model can now do web search
4. **`orchestrator.go`** — Simplify search sub-agent creation (no separate search model needed)
5. **`cmd/server/main.go`** — Remove `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var

### WebSearch Tool Config

```go
type WebSearch struct {
    SearchContextSize string   // "low", "medium" (default), "high"
    AllowedDomains    []string // optional domain filter
}
```

### Simplification

The search sub-agent no longer needs a separate model. When `provider=openai`, both the data agent and search agent can use the same model — web search is just another tool in the `tools` array. This eliminates the `OpenAISearchModel` config and the second `NewLLMModel()` call in `BuildAgent`.

# Design: Migrate OpenAI Adapter to Responses API

**Date:** 2026-03-04
**Status:** Approved

## Problem

The current OpenAI adapter uses the Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) with `web_search_options: {}` for web search. This has limitations:

- Requires a dedicated search-capable model (`gpt-5-search-api`) — separate from the main model
- No control over search parameters (domain filtering, search context size, user location)
- Chat Completions search models are being superseded by the Responses API

## Solution

Migrate the OpenAI adapter from Chat Completions to the Responses API (`/v1/responses`), which:

- Supports `web_search` as a native tool alongside function tools
- Works with any supported model (gpt-5, gpt-4.1, o4-mini, etc.)
- Provides domain filtering (`allowed_domains`), `search_context_size`, and `user_location`
- Eliminates the need for a separate search model

## Scope

### Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/agent/openai/openai.go` | Rewrite request/response types and `doRequest()` for Responses API |
| `internal/agent/openai/openai_test.go` | Update tests for new wire format |
| `internal/agent/openai/websearch.go` | Add configurable fields (SearchContextSize, AllowedDomains) |
| `internal/agent/model.go` | Remove `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig` |
| `internal/agent/orchestrator.go` | Simplify search sub-agent (same model, no separate model creation) |
| `cmd/server/main.go` | Remove `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var |

### Request Format (Before → After)

**Before (Chat Completions):**
```json
{
  "model": "gpt-5.2",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "..."}],
  "tools": [{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_releases", ...}}],
  "web_search_options": {}
}
```

**After (Responses):**
```json
{
  "model": "gpt-5.2",
  "input": [{"role": "user", "content": "..."}],
  "tools": [
    {"type": "function", "name": "get_releases", "parameters": {...}},
    {"type": "web_search", "search_context_size": "medium"}
  ]
}
```

### Response Format (Before → After)

**Before:** `choices[0].message.content` + `choices[0].message.tool_calls`

**After:** `output` array containing:
- `{type: "web_search_call", status: "completed"}` — search invocation record
- `{type: "function_call", id, name, arguments}` — function tool calls
- `{type: "message", content: [{type: "output_text", text, annotations}]}` — final text with citations

### WebSearch Tool Enhancement

```go
type WebSearch struct {
    SearchContextSize string   // "low", "medium" (default), "high"
    AllowedDomains    []string // optional domain filter
}
```

The `ProcessRequest` method will encode these fields into the tool config rather than using the GoogleSearch marker hack.

### Config Simplification

**Removed from LLMConfig:**
- `OpenAISearchModel` — no longer needed; any model supports web search via tools

**Removed env vars:**
- `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` — search model is the same as the main model

### Orchestrator Simplification

The search sub-agent no longer needs a separate model. In `BuildAgent()`:

```go
// Before: separate model for search
searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, LLMConfig{
    Provider: "openai", Model: "gpt-5-search-api", ...
})

// After: same model, web search is just a tool
searchAgent = llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
    Model: llmModel,  // same model as root/data agent
    Tools: []tool.Tool{oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}},
})
```

## Non-Goals

- Streaming support (not implemented today, stays that way)
- Deep research models (o3-deep-research etc.)
- Gemini adapter changes (unaffected)

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown

input.prompt:
Show me the exact format for sending function call results back in the Responses API multi-turn flow. Specifically:

1. When the model returns function_call items, how do I send results back? Do I need to echo the function_call item AND add a function_call_output, or just the output?
2. Is there a "previous_response_id" approach that avoids re-sending the full history?
3. What is the exact format of the function_call_output input item?

content.content:
## Responses API Function Calling: Multi-Turn Format

### 1. Echoing function_call items + adding outputs

Yes, you need **both**. The docs show appending the entire `response.output` (which includes function_call items), then adding output items:

```python
input_list += response.output

for item in response.output:
    if item.type == "function_call":
        input_list.append({
            "type": "function_call_output",
            "call_id": item.call_id,
            "output": json.dumps({"horoscope": horoscope})
        })
```

The pattern is: accumulate a running input list, append all output items from the response, then append your `function_call_output` entries matched by `call_id`.

### 2. `previous_response_id` approach

The page content provided does not mention a `previous_response_id` parameter for the Responses API function calling flow. The documented approach uses a "running input list" that you build up over time.

### 3. Exact format of `function_call_output`

```json
{
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_12345xyz",
    "output": "{\"temperature\": \"25\", \"unit\": \"C\"}"
}
```

The `output` field is typically a string (JSON-encoded or plain text). For functions returning images/files, you can pass "an array of image or file objects instead of a string."

### 4. Tool definition structure in Responses API

In the Responses API, `description` is at the **top level** alongside `type` and `name` — there is **no** nested `function` key. Compare:

**Responses API format:**
```json
{
    "type": "function",
    "name": "get_horoscope",
    "description": "Get today's horoscope for an astrological sign.",
    "parameters": { ... }
}
```

**Chat Completions API format** (different — has nested `function` key):
```json
{
    "type": "function",
    "function": {
        "name": "get_horoscope",
        "description": "Get today's horoscope...",
        "parameters": { ... },
        "strict": true
    }
}
```

This is a key structural difference between the two APIs.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/subagent-driven-development

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ❌ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: ✅ Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)

**Quality gates:**
- Self-review catches issues before handoff
- Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality
- Review loops ensure fixes actually work
- Spec compliance prevents over/under-building
- Code quality ensures implementation is well-built

**Cost:**
- More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task)
- Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront)
- Review loops add iterations
- But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later)

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent
- Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality)
- Proceed with unfixed issues
- Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts)
- Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead)
- Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits)
- Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed)
- Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done)
- Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again)
- Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed)
- **Start code quality review before spec compliance is ✅** (wrong order)
- Move to next task while either review has open issues

**If subagent asks questions:**
- Answer clearly and completely
- Provide additional context if needed
- Don't rush them into implementation

**If reviewer finds issues:**
- Implementer (same subagent) fixes them
- Reviewer reviews again
- Repeat until approved
- Don't skip the re-review

**If subagent fails task:**
- Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions
- Don't try to fix manually (context pollution)

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

**Subagents should use:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task

**Alternative workflow:**
- **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution

  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |

    - The requirements or acceptance criteria
    - The approach or implementation strategy

    - Did I miss any requirements?
    - Are there edge cases I didn't handle?

    - Are names clear and accurate (match what things do, not how they work)?

    **Discipline:**
    - Did I avoid overbuilding (YAGNI)?

    **Testing:**

    - Did I follow TDD if required?
    - Are tests comprehensive?

// --- Responses API types ---

type responsesRequest struct {
	Model        string         `json:"model"`
	Input        []inputItem    `json:"input"`
	Instructions string         `json:"instructions,omitempty"`
	Tools        []responseTool `json:"tools,omitempty"`
	Temperature  *float32       `json:"temperature,omitempty"`
	TopP         *float32       `json:"top_p,omitempty"`
	MaxTokens    int32          `json:"max_output_tokens,omitempty"`
	Store        bool           `json:"store"`
}

type inputItem struct {
	Type      string `json:"type"`               // "message", "function_call", "function_call_output"
	Role      string `json:"role,omitempty"`
	Content   string `json:"content,omitempty"`
	ID        string `json:"id,omitempty"`
	CallID    string `json:"call_id,omitempty"`
	Name      string `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Arguments string `json:"arguments,omitempty"`
	Output    string `json:"output,omitempty"`
}

type responseTool struct {
	Type              string         `json:"type"` // "function" or "web_search"
	Name              string         `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Description       string         `json:"description,omitempty"`
	Parameters        any            `json:"parameters,omitempty"`
	SearchContextSize string         `json:"search_context_size,omitempty"`
	UserLocation      any            `json:"user_location,omitempty"`
	Filters           *searchFilters `json:"filters,omitempty"`
}

type searchFilters struct {
	AllowedDomains []string `json:"allowed_domains,omitempty"`
}

type responsesResponse struct {
	ID         string       `json:"id"`
	Status     string       `json:"status"`
	Output     []outputItem `json:"output"`
	OutputText string       `json:"output_text"`
	Usage      *respUsage   `json:"usage,omitempty"`
	Error      *apiError    `json:"error,omitempty"`
}

type outputItem struct {
	Type      string          `json:"type"` // "message", "function_call", "web_search_call"
	ID        string          `json:"id,omitempty"`
	Status    string          `json:"status,omitempty"`
	CallID    string          `json:"call_id,omitempty"`
	Name      string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Arguments string          `json:"arguments,omitempty"`
	Role      string          `json:"role,omitempty"`
	Content   []outputContent `json:"content,omitempty"`
}

type outputContent struct {
	Type        string       `json:"type"` // "output_text"
	Text        string       `json:"text"`
	Annotations []annotation `json:"annotations,omitempty"`
}

type annotation struct {
	Type       string `json:"type"` // "url_citation"
	URL        string `json:"url"`
	Title      string `json:"title"`
	StartIndex int    `json:"start_index"`
	EndIndex   int    `json:"end_index"`
}

type respUsage struct {
	InputTokens  int32 `json:"input_tokens"`
	OutputTokens int32 `json:"output_tokens"`
	TotalTokens  int32 `json:"total_tokens"`
}

type apiError struct {
	Message string `json:"message"`
	Type    string `json:"type"`
	Code    string `json:"code"`
}
